tasks.title: Fix current example chat clone embed regression
tasks.summary: Fix cloned example chats so embeds survive the transition from in-memory example chat data into the real cloned chat.
tasks.reason_retained: The latest failed-test report still lists example-chat-clone.spec.ts failing because the source example chat had embeds before cloning and the cloned chat had zero embeds.
acceptance_criteria: Investigate the latest example-chat-clone.spec.ts report and screenshots.
Fix cloned example chats so embeds survive cloning.
Preserve shared/example chat embed rendering behavior.
Verify with the relevant Playwright spec via the repo test runner.

tasks.title: Generate custom OG images for shared chats
tasks.summary: Replace the generic shared-chat Open Graph image with a privacy-safe per-chat OG image strategy.
tasks.reason_retained: The shared-chat route still falls back to /images/og-image.jpg, so the core request has not been implemented.
acceptance_criteria: Design a privacy-safe per-chat OG image strategy that does not expose decrypted chat content to the server unless explicitly intended.
Generate or cache an OG image for shared chats using safe metadata such as title, category, or icon, or document why a generic image is required.
Ensure the shared-chat route emits the generated image URL in og:image and twitter:image.
Add or update tests/docs for the shared-chat OG metadata flow.

tasks.title: Fix current reminder E2E failures
tasks.summary: Fix the current reminder settings/redesign regressions shown by the latest daily Playwright failures.
tasks.reason_retained: The latest failed-test report still lists reminder-button-settings.spec.ts and reminder-redesign.spec.ts failures around missing reminder item/success UI.
acceptance_criteria: Investigate the latest reminder failure reports and screenshots.
Fix the current reminder UI/message/test regressions.
Preserve existing reminder creation, settings, and redesign behavior.
Verify the relevant reminder specs via the repo test runner.

tasks.title: Evaluate Vercel to Hetzner VM migration for frontend hosting
tasks.summary: Evaluate whether moving the SvelteKit frontend from Vercel to a Hetzner VM would improve cost control without unacceptable performance or maintenance tradeoffs.
tasks.reason_retained: The repo still documents Vercel deployment as the active frontend path, so the hosting-cost evaluation has not been superseded by code or docs.
acceptance_criteria: Compare Vercel Pro cost limits with a Hetzner VM plus Caddy or nginx deployment.
Evaluate adapter-vercel versus adapter-node implications for SSR and prerendering.
Assess CDN and edge-performance tradeoffs.
Estimate migration effort and operational risk.

tasks.title: Research alternative providers for image search
tasks.summary: Evaluate whether Flickr Commons and Wikidata/Wikimedia media APIs should complement the current Brave image search and SerpAPI Google Lens providers.
tasks.reason_retained: The image search skill currently documents and implements Brave text image search plus SerpAPI Google Lens reverse search; Flickr/Wikidata image-provider support was not found, but the related research is older than eight weeks.
acceptance_criteria: Compare Flickr Commons, Wikidata/Wikimedia media, and existing Brave/SerpAPI coverage for licensing, result quality, privacy, quota, and cost.
Document whether each provider should be added to images/search, daily inspirations, or neither.
If a provider is recommended, define the shared provider wrapper, privacy-policy impact, and test coverage needed before implementation.
Avoid adding a provider unless it improves result quality or licensing coverage beyond the current providers.

tasks.title: Improve document embed prompt preselection
tasks.summary: Improve preprocessing so document-generation instructions are loaded reliably when the user asks to create or edit a document embed.
tasks.reason_retained: Document embeds and document-generation instructions exist, but the issue is older than eight weeks and still points to a plausible preprocessor gap around when docs/document embed instructions are selected.
acceptance_criteria: Identify the prompts and preprocessor signals that cause document embed instructions to be included.
Add or refine detection for user requests to create documents, reports, formatted files, or document embeds.
Keep prompt loading gated enough to avoid injecting document instructions into unrelated chats.
Add focused coverage for a document-creation request that previously missed the docs/document embed path.

tasks.title: Migrate focus mode system prompts to per-language Markdown files
tasks.summary: Move long multilingual focus-mode system prompts out of YAML string blocks into per-language Markdown files while preserving translation output semantics.
tasks.reason_retained: Focus mode prompts are still resolved through `focus_modes.*.systemprompt` translation keys and YAML/i18n sources; no per-language Markdown prompt pipeline was found.
acceptance_criteria: Design a source layout for per-focus-mode, per-language Markdown prompt files.
Update the translation build pipeline so generated locale JSON keeps the same `focus_modes.*.systemprompt` values.
Provide a migration script for existing YAML systemprompt blocks.
Verify resolved prompt text remains equivalent after migration, excluding intentional whitespace normalization.

tasks.title: Fix current FixtureRecorder inline code embed replay regression
tasks.summary: Fix the current code-generation mock replay path so inline code embeds produced by recorded LLM responses render with valid embed IDs during replay.
tasks.reason_retained: The latest failed-test report still lists code-generation-multiturn.spec.ts failing, and the fixture/replay system is still a likely area because it rewrites recorded embed IDs during mock replay.
acceptance_criteria: Investigate the latest code-generation-multiturn.spec.ts failure report and screenshots.
Confirm whether recorded inline code embed payloads are captured and recreated correctly during replay.
Fix replay so response markers point at fresh embeds that the frontend can resolve.
Verify code-generation-multiturn.spec.ts through the repo test runner.

tasks.title: Reduce first-message cold-start latency after backend restart
tasks.summary: Profile and reduce the extra latency seen by the first AI message after API or worker restart.
tasks.reason_retained: Startup preloading exists for several caches, but the backend still contains multiple first-request fallback paths and this older performance task has not been conclusively resolved by code or docs.
acceptance_criteria: Profile the first AI message after a backend restart and compare it with a follow-up message.
Identify the top first-request latency sources across API, worker, cache, provider clients, and config loading.
Move safe warmup work into FastAPI lifespan startup or Celery worker boot.
Document remaining lazy-init paths that are intentionally deferred.

tasks.title: Design batch task expansion for one task per list entry
tasks.summary: Design a UI and execution model for turning a list into multiple focused chats or tasks, one per entry, with explicit progress tracking.
tasks.reason_retained: No dedicated batch task expansion UI or `/btw` command implementation was found, and the idea remains relevant to multi-step agentic workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Define user flows for selecting a list and creating one task/chat per entry.
Decide whether the feature belongs in chat, tasks, sub-chats, or the future plan view.
Specify how progress, failures, and per-entry outputs are displayed.
Document safeguards that prevent the assistant from skipping entries.

tasks.title: Add weekly personalized inspiration newsletter
tasks.summary: Create an opt-in weekly email digest that curates personalized daily-inspiration-style content for each user.
tasks.reason_retained: Newsletter infrastructure and daily inspiration infrastructure exist, but no weekly personalized inspiration digest implementation was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define the opt-in setting and unsubscribe behavior for a weekly inspiration digest.
Reuse daily inspiration data where appropriate without sending an uncurated dump of seven daily items.
Create localized responsive email content using the existing newsletter/email stack.
Schedule delivery through the existing backend task infrastructure and document privacy implications.

tasks.title: Add proactive next-task selection workflow
tasks.summary: Design and implement a workflow that can select, plan, and work through eligible tasks one at a time with minimal user input while preserving user control.
tasks.reason_retained: A `next-tasks` skill exists, but no full product workflow for proactive task selection and processing was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define eligibility rules for tasks that can be planned or implemented with minimal user input.
Require clarification or confirmation before risky, ambiguous, sensitive, or high-impact work.
Integrate with the existing task-management/session workflow rather than creating a parallel tracker.
Document how the workflow reports progress and stops safely after each task.

tasks.title: Add anti-scam filtering guidance for home search results
tasks.summary: Improve home/apartment search responses so obvious scammy listings are filtered or flagged and users receive safety guidance when searching housing listings.
tasks.reason_retained: The Home app search instruction covers provider usage and filters, but no explicit apartment-scam filtering or renter safety guidance was found.
acceptance_criteria: Add guidance for identifying obvious apartment listing scams or spam in home search results.
Ensure the assistant flags suspicious results instead of presenting them as normal recommendations.
Include concise safety advice for housing searches without overwhelming legitimate results.
Add or update coverage for a home-search response that includes suspicious listing patterns.

tasks.title: Implement production auto-deploy with minimal downtime
tasks.summary: Implement the planned host-level production deploy agent so main-branch deployments are automated, verified, and minimize downtime.
tasks.reason_retained: An architecture document for auto-deploy exists, but the planned deploy-agent implementation files were not found under `deployment/`.
acceptance_criteria: Implement a host-level deploy agent or equivalent production-safe deployment controller.
Verify GitHub webhook signatures and restrict deploys to the intended branch.
Build before swapping running containers and run post-deploy health checks.
Validate and reload Caddy configuration as part of the deploy flow.
Document setup, rollback, and operational failure modes.

tasks.title: Implement semantic chat search with encrypted embeddings
tasks.summary: Add semantic chat search that stores encrypted chat embedding vectors and performs similarity search client-side.
tasks.reason_retained: `docs/architecture/frontend/semantic-chat-search.md` is still marked planned and current chat search remains substring-oriented.
acceptance_criteria: Add encrypted embedding storage for chat summaries/tags without server-side vector search over user data.
Generate or update embeddings during existing postprocessing or a documented background path.
Sync encrypted vectors through the chat metadata flow and decrypt them client-side.
Show semantic results alongside exact substring matches with clear ranking behavior.
Document model choice, privacy impact, and backfill strategy.

tasks.title: Add folders for app settings and memories
tasks.summary: Add first-class grouping or folder support for app settings and memories so users can organize saved personal data beyond the current `settings_group` field.
tasks.reason_retained: Settings and memories entries have a `settings_group` category, but no user-managed folder/group organization workflow was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define the encrypted data model for user-managed settings/memory groups or folders.
Add UI for creating, renaming, moving, and deleting groups without exposing decrypted content to the server.
Preserve existing app category filtering and mention/deep-link behavior.
Include import/export behavior for grouped settings and memories.

tasks.title: Unify provider routing metadata across app skills
tasks.summary: Create a shared provider-routing model for country, category, scope, and provider compatibility across skills instead of duplicating logic per app.
tasks.reason_retained: Provider routing exists in individual apps such as events, shopping, travel, payments, and frontend metadata, but no unified cross-skill provider registry was found.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory provider routing needs across apps with country, category, scope, and opt-in constraints.
Define shared provider metadata that can serve backend routing, frontend display, privacy policy, and health checks.
Migrate at least one app from bespoke routing to the shared model as a proof of concept.
Document how providers are added, disabled, and selected.

tasks.title: Fix Siegessaeule event provider timeout and opt-in routing
tasks.summary: Make the Siegessaeule events provider safe to re-enable by fixing timeout hierarchy and only selecting it for Berlin LGBTQ+ event intent.
tasks.reason_retained: The events provider registry still excludes Siegessaeule from auto-select with a comment pointing at the unresolved timeout and opt-in routing work.
acceptance_criteria: Ensure Siegessaeule provider timeouts cannot exceed the enclosing skill executor timeout.
Keep Siegessaeule opt-in or intent-gated for Berlin LGBTQ+ event searches.
Re-enable it only when slow proxy responses do not fail unrelated event searches.
Add provider routing and timeout coverage for Berlin LGBTQ+ and generic Berlin event queries.

tasks.title: Optimize base AI instructions for conciseness and on-demand loading
tasks.summary: Reduce always-loaded AI base instructions by moving optional guidance behind clearer runtime gates where safe.
tasks.reason_retained: `main_processor.py` still appends many base instructions on most turns, with only some instruction families gated by app availability, selected tools, or embed history.
acceptance_criteria: Measure current base-instruction token cost across representative chat flows.
Identify instructions that can be loaded only when a relevant skill, embed type, focus mode, or user intent is present.
Avoid removing safety, privacy, or required tool-contract instructions from flows that need them.
Document the gating rules and add regression coverage for at least one newly gated instruction.

tasks.title: Implement parallel execution for independent app skill calls
tasks.summary: Execute independent tool or app skill calls in parallel where safe instead of processing every tool call sequentially.
tasks.reason_retained: The main processor still iterates through tool calls sequentially after validation, aside from per-skill support for multiple requests.
acceptance_criteria: Define which tool calls are safe to execute in parallel and which must remain ordered or exclusive.
Preserve budget accounting, billing, cancellation, placeholder embed handling, and focus-mode exclusivity.
Execute independent app skills concurrently and merge tool results deterministically into message history.
Add tests covering parallel success, one-call failure, budget limits, and cancellation behavior.

tasks.title: Add academic-integrity safeguards against paper-mill misuse
tasks.summary: Research and implement guidance that reduces misuse of OpenMates for paper mills, fabricated academic work, and reliance on retracted or unreliable science papers.
tasks.reason_retained: AI preprocessing currently has generic harmful and misuse-risk scoring, and learning mode mentions plagiarism reduction, but no dedicated academic-integrity or retracted-paper safeguard was found. The task is older than eight weeks and still relevant as product-safety work.
acceptance_criteria: Research paper-mill and retracted-paper misuse patterns relevant to OpenMates research, writing, and science flows.
Define policy guidance for acceptable learning/research assistance versus academic fraud, fabricated citations, or ghostwritten submissions.
Update AI instructions, preprocessing, or app-specific guidance where needed without blocking legitimate study and research use.
Add coverage for at least one academic-integrity misuse request and one legitimate research-assistance request.
Document any user-facing refusal or redirection behavior for academic-integrity cases.

tasks.title: Implement virtualized chat message rendering
tasks.summary: Reduce long-chat memory and DOM pressure by virtualizing chat history and destroying offscreen read-only editors safely.
tasks.reason_retained: `docs/architecture/frontend/virtual-scrolling-chat-history.md` still documents the planned work, while `ChatHistory.svelte` still renders all `displayMessages` in a plain keyed `{#each}` and no `messageHeightCache.ts` utility exists.
acceptance_criteria: Make `ReadOnlyMessage.svelte` release TipTap editors when far outside the viewport and recreate from cached content when needed.
Add height-cache infrastructure for rich messages, embeds, code blocks, and images.
Replace full chat-history rendering with a stable windowed renderer that preserves scroll-to-bottom and streaming spacer behavior.
Verify long conversations, streaming messages, selection menus, embeds, and mobile sidebar-closed chat flows.

tasks.title: Normalize mobile date and time settings fields
tasks.summary: Make date and time inputs in settings match canonical settings dropdown/input height and show useful placeholder or empty-state text on mobile.
tasks.reason_retained: `SettingsInput.svelte` supports native `date` and `time` types but applies generic text-input styling; reminder settings still use native date/time fields directly, so the mobile visual mismatch remains plausible and older than eight weeks.
acceptance_criteria: Audit date and time fields in reminder/settings UI on mobile viewports.
Align native date/time input height, padding, typography, and radius with canonical settings inputs/dropdowns.
Provide clear placeholder or empty-state text for date/time fields where native controls hide placeholders.
Add or update mobile coverage for the affected reminder/settings fields.

tasks.title: Improve agent clarification workflow for long-running tasks
tasks.summary: Design how agentic workflows can leave progress comments and request clarifying input with a notification instead of silently stalling.
tasks.reason_retained: Sub-chat clarification exists through `ask_user_input`, and notification infrastructure exists elsewhere, but no cohesive user-facing workflow was found for progress comments plus clarification notifications during long-running agent work.
acceptance_criteria: Define when an agent should pause for clarification instead of continuing or guessing.
Design a progress-comment surface for task/chat workflows that does not spam users.
Add a notification path for important clarification requests while respecting notification settings.
Document cancellation, timeout, and resume behavior for paused agent tasks.

tasks.title: Add urgent assistant notification emails
tasks.summary: Allow explicitly approved workflows to send urgent email notifications that are separate from routine chat notifications.
tasks.reason_retained: The repo has internal/test/docs notification infrastructure and offline webhook email notifications, but no user-facing skill or permissioned workflow for urgent assistant-triggered notification emails was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define which workflows may request urgent email notifications and what user consent is required.
Separate urgent notifications from regular chat/webhook email preferences.
Add rate limits, audit logging, and abuse protections for assistant-triggered emails.
Provide localized user-facing copy for opt-in, send confirmation, and failure states.

tasks.title: Add a health follow-up focus mode
tasks.summary: Create a health-focused mode for recurring follow-up chats around topics such as sleep issues, sleep hygiene, and ongoing wellness tracking.
tasks.reason_retained: Health app and health focus modes exist for appointments, reports, and insights, but no dedicated recurring follow-up or sleep-hygiene mode was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define the scope of a recurring health follow-up mode without replacing professional medical advice.
Add focus-mode metadata, prompts, summaries, and localized settings/app-store copy.
Support follow-up reminders or suggested next check-ins where appropriate.
Verify safety disclaimers and handoff language for medical concerns.

tasks.title: Import settings and memories from text files
tasks.summary: Let users import structured or semi-structured text into settings and memories, including examples such as exported favorite places.
tasks.reason_retained: Settings/memories and file upload flows exist, but no user-facing import workflow for settings and memories from a text file was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define supported file formats and parsing rules for settings/memory import.
Preview extracted entries and require user confirmation before saving encrypted memories.
Support common exported text/list use cases such as favorite places from mapping tools.
Document privacy behavior and add coverage for import, duplicate handling, and cancel paths.

tasks.title: Improve daily meeting server stats data quality
tasks.summary: Fix remaining daily meeting server-stat data gaps and expand health metrics once web, app, and signup-funnel data flows are reliable.
tasks.reason_retained: Server stats, web analytics, app analytics, and signup funnel aggregation now exist, but recent logs still show web analytics Directus update failures and the original data-gap surfaces remain worth tracking as older backend work.
acceptance_criteria: Verify web analytics counters flush correctly to `web_analytics_daily` without repeated Directus update failures.
Verify app analytics events aggregate into `app_analytics_daily` with useful per-app/skill dimensions.
Verify signup funnel rows populate and expose conversion rates.
Add daily meeting output for top app usage, funnel conversion, web analytics, trends, and month-to-date aggregates once data is reliable.

tasks.title: Implement updated chat settings sections
tasks.summary: Bring the chat settings menu closer to the planned files, usage, share, and tasks settings design while preserving existing settings navigation patterns.
tasks.reason_retained: Share and usage settings components exist, but a complete updated chat settings menu covering files and tasks settings from the design references was not found.
acceptance_criteria: Audit current chat settings against the planned files, usage, share, and tasks sections.
Use only canonical settings components and avoid custom inline settings CSS.
Implement missing sections incrementally, starting with the highest-value section.
Add mobile and desktop coverage for opening each settings section from an active chat.

tasks.title: Audit LLM provider usage extraction and token fallbacks
tasks.summary: Ensure every LLM provider extracts real API usage where available and uses logged token estimation only as an emergency fallback.
tasks.reason_retained: Provider clients still vary in usage handling; Groq and Cerebras paths lack the same explicit logged fallback pattern as OpenAI/Mistral when API usage is absent.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory all provider clients under `backend/apps/ai/llm_providers/`.
Verify each provider extracts official API usage for streaming and non-streaming responses where supported.
Add logged token-estimation fallback only when official usage is genuinely missing.
Extend provider usage extraction tests so silent zero usage cannot regress.

tasks.title: Build BYOC Claude subscription worker mode
tasks.summary: Let users connect a local Claude Code CLI subscription as an optional LLM worker through the OpenMates CLI remote-access architecture.
tasks.reason_retained: `docs/architecture/apps/cli-remote-access.md` documents Claude Worker Mode as planned, but no implementation wiring for `openmates remote-access start --claude-worker` was found.
acceptance_criteria: Implement CLI worker registration and lifecycle for `--claude-worker`.
Add backend routing/model-selection support for user-owned local workers with fallback to server-side providers when unavailable.
Stream output, usage metadata, errors, and cancellation through the existing remote-access protocol.
Document security, privacy, billing display, and offline behavior.

tasks.title: Design the Finance app foundation
tasks.summary: Plan and implement a provider-agnostic Finance app foundation for Revolut Business and InvoiceNinja-backed finance workflows.
tasks.reason_retained: Only the finance mate and payment-side Revolut/InvoiceNinja infrastructure were found; no `backend/apps/finance` app or user-facing finance skills were found.
acceptance_criteria: Define the Finance app manifest, skill boundaries, and privacy model.
Split connected accounts, review, exchange-rate, invoice, and send-money work into implementable phases.
Reuse shared providers and avoid skill-specific provider coupling.
Document risk controls for financial data, confirmations, and provider credentials.

tasks.title: Implement connected accounts for Finance providers
tasks.summary: Add encrypted per-user connected accounts so finance skills can use Revolut Business and InvoiceNinja credentials without exposing secrets to the LLM.
tasks.reason_retained: `docs/architecture/core/security.md` marks connected accounts as planned, and no `connected_accounts` settings/memory implementation or Revolut Business shared provider was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define encrypted `connected_accounts` schema and settings UI for provider connections.
Strip credentials before LLM context while exposing only safe account display metadata.
Resolve full credentials inside skill execution from encrypted storage or approved cache paths.
Add Revolut Business and InvoiceNinja provider connection health checks and disconnect behavior.

tasks.title: Add Finance create-invoice skill
tasks.summary: Create invoices through InvoiceNinja from a user-confirmed Finance skill flow, including client lookup, line items, and optional emailing.
tasks.reason_retained: InvoiceNinja service modules can create invoices, but no user-facing Finance create-invoice skill, connected-account credential path, invoice embed, or email-invoice helper was found.
acceptance_criteria: Resolve clients by name or email and create missing clients only after user confirmation.
Create invoices with line items and defaults from encrypted finance settings/memories.
Support optional invoice email sending when the provider API and user confirmation allow it.
Render invoice results in a dedicated embed or reusable finance preview.

tasks.title: Add Finance send-money skill
tasks.summary: Design and implement a high-safety Revolut Business transfer flow with preview, explicit confirmation, idempotency, and audit logging.
tasks.reason_retained: No Revolut Business shared provider or Finance send-money skill was found; existing Revolut code is payment-side SEPA transfer handling, not user-initiated transfers.
acceptance_criteria: Implement counterparty search/creation and source-account resolution through connected accounts.
Require a two-phase preview and explicit confirmation before any transfer or draft is created.
Use idempotency, audit logging, amount/currency validation, and clear pending/completed/draft statuses.
Add sandbox tests and document fraud, mistake-prevention, and recovery behavior.

tasks.title: Research LLM-generated finance report templates
tasks.summary: Evaluate a secure template system where users can create reusable finance report logic from metadata and run it against real transaction data in a sandbox.
tasks.reason_retained: The Finance app is not implemented yet, and no E2B-backed finance report template workflow was found; the idea remains relevant as a follow-up after the finance foundation.
acceptance_criteria: Define whether report templates are a separate skill or part of Finance review.
Design encrypted template storage, editing, and deletion behavior.
Evaluate sandbox execution and data-minimization for generated Python code over financial data.
Prototype at least one report template flow after core Finance skills exist.

tasks.title: Research Treatwell as a beauty appointment provider
tasks.summary: Evaluate adding a beauty/wellness appointment app or provider backed by Treatwell or similar salon-booking sources.
tasks.reason_retained: Health appointment search exists for medical appointments, and shopping has a generic beauty category, but no beauty appointment app, Treatwell provider, or salon-booking workflow was found.
acceptance_criteria: Research Treatwell provider feasibility, privacy impact, locale coverage, and anti-bot constraints.
Decide whether beauty appointments should be a new app or a non-medical provider category under an existing app.
Define the search request schema and embed type needed for salon/beauty bookings.
Document whether implementation should proceed or be rejected due to provider, privacy, or product-fit constraints.

tasks.title: Expand concert and classical music event search
tasks.summary: Improve event search coverage for concerts, classical music, and related cultural events beyond the current Berlin Philharmonic-specific path.
tasks.reason_retained: Events search supports general providers and a Berlin Philharmonic provider with concert tags, but broader concert/classical music provider coverage and routing were not found.
acceptance_criteria: Audit current concert coverage across Eventbrite, Google Events, Resident Advisor, and Berlin Philharmonic.
Identify provider gaps for classical music, concert halls, and ticketed cultural events in target regions.
Add or document provider routing so concert queries select the right sources without narrowing generic event search.
Verify representative classical and non-classical concert searches.

tasks.title: Offer annotate/edit mode after image upload
tasks.summary: Show a lightweight affordance to annotate or edit a user-uploaded image directly after it is added to the message input.
tasks.reason_retained: Sketch/drawing and uploaded-image fullscreen components exist, but no quick link from a newly uploaded image to annotate or edit mode was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define the UX for offering annotation only when an uploaded image can be edited safely.
Connect uploaded-image previews to an annotate/edit flow without losing the original image.
Persist annotated output as a separate embed or attachment with clear provenance.
Verify mobile touch drawing, undo/cancel, and send-message flows.

tasks.title: Add location-based reminders
tasks.summary: Let users create reminders that trigger when they are in or near a specified city or place, with privacy-preserving location handling.
tasks.reason_retained: Geolocation exists in the map input flow and time-based reminders exist, but no location-triggered reminder model or reminder UI was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define whether location reminders are web-only, mobile-first, or native-app-only based on reliable background location capabilities.
Design consent, precision, retention, and privacy behavior for location matching.
Add reminder schema support for place/city/geofence triggers if feasible.
Provide clear fallback behavior when browser or OS background location is unavailable.

tasks.title: Add read-later and watch-later actions for embeds
tasks.summary: Extend embed fullscreen actions so users can save websites, videos, and similar embeds to a read/watch-later list instead of only saving some embeds as memories.
tasks.reason_retained: Many fullscreen embeds support `Add memory`, but no dedicated read-later/watch-later action or queue was found for web and video embeds.
acceptance_criteria: Define how read-later/watch-later differs from saved embed memories.
Add actions to relevant fullscreen embed types such as websites and videos.
Create a settings or app surface where saved read/watch-later items can be reviewed and removed.
Preserve encrypted embed references and avoid exposing private content to the server.

tasks.title: Add a creative writing game mode
tasks.summary: Create a playful creative-writing focus mode or skill that guides users through interactive story prompts and writing exercises.
tasks.reason_retained: Creative and writing-related mates/settings exist, but no dedicated creative-writing game focus mode or app skill was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define the core loop for the writing game, including prompts, turns, scoring or feedback, and stop conditions.
Decide whether it belongs as a focus mode, app skill, or both.
Add localized metadata, prompt guidance, and app-store/settings copy.
Verify it does not conflict with normal writing/editing flows.

tasks.title: Create correction documents with highlighted changes
tasks.summary: When users ask for text correction, generate a document embed that highlights changed text and exposes the original text on hover or click.
tasks.reason_retained: Document embeds and message highlight overlays exist, but no text-correction flow that creates a diff-style document embed with original-vs-corrected highlights was found.
acceptance_criteria: Define the document model for corrected text, inserted/deleted spans, and original text annotations.
Trigger the document embed path for suitable text-correction requests without forcing it for short inline corrections.
Render highlighted changes accessibly in preview/fullscreen, including keyboard and touch interactions.
Add coverage for correction requests with replacements, deletions, and unchanged paragraphs.

tasks.title: Improve app skill filtering for richer user constraints
tasks.summary: Make app skills better at handling complex filters and spatial constraints, such as searching for cafes between two Berlin districts.
tasks.reason_retained: Several skills support structured filters and coordinates, but no shared approach was found for route-between, midpoint, or richer post-filtering constraints across provider-backed skills.
acceptance_criteria: Identify high-value constraint types that current skills handle poorly, including between-two-places, route-adjacent, opening-hours, and multi-attribute filters.
Decide what belongs in LLM request normalization versus provider-side filtering versus local post-processing.
Implement one representative skill improvement as a proof of concept.
Document reusable patterns for adding richer parameters to future skills.

tasks.title: Move AI ask settings into chat settings where appropriate
tasks.summary: Reduce user confusion by surfacing everyday AI ask settings in chat settings while keeping developer/API/CLI settings in the AI app where they belong.
tasks.reason_retained: AI ask settings still exist as app/skill settings components, so the user-facing settings placement concern remains relevant despite newer settings architecture.
acceptance_criteria: Audit which AI ask settings are chat-user preferences versus API/CLI/developer configuration.
Move or duplicate only the user-facing controls that users expect to find in chat settings.
Keep canonical settings components and avoid splitting one source of truth for persisted settings.
Verify settings deep links and mobile settings navigation still work.

tasks.title: Audit architecture docs for stale implementation claims
tasks.summary: Systematically extract implementation claims from architecture docs, compare them with current code, and fix stale or misleading documentation.
tasks.reason_retained: `sessions.py stale-docs` currently reports many stale architecture docs, and existing doc tools check freshness but do not fully extract and verify implementation claims from each document.
acceptance_criteria: Prioritize stale docs with security, privacy, messaging, AI, and frontend architecture impact.
Extract concrete claims about implemented, planned, or unsupported behavior from each selected doc.
Verify claims against current code and tests, then update docs or create follow-up tasks for real gaps.
Add a repeatable workflow or script output so future stale-doc reviews are easier.
